params.name: added_date
params.description: Show images added on the specified date

params.name: added_date_start
params.description: Show images added on or after the specified date

params.name: aspect_ratio_min
params.description: Show images with the specified aspect ratio or higher, using a positive decimal of the width divided by the height, such as 1.7778 for a 16:9 image

params.name: aspect_ratio_max
params.description: Show images with the specified aspect ratio or lower, using a positive decimal of the width divided by the height, such as 1.7778 for a 16:9 image

params.name: aspect_ratio
params.description: Show images with the specified aspect ratio, using a positive decimal of the width divided by the height, such as 1.7778 for a 16:9 image

params.name: added_date_end
params.description: Show images added before the specified date

params.name: category
params.description: Show images with the specified Shutterstock-defined category; specify a category name or ID

params.name: color
params.description: Specify either a hexadecimal color in the format '4F21EA' or 'grayscale'; the API groups it into one of 15 color categories and returns images that primarily use that color category

params.name: contributor
params.description: Show images with the specified contributor names or IDs, allows multiple

params.name: contributor_country
params.description: Show images from contributors in one or more specified countries

params.name: fields
params.description: Fields to display in the response; see the documentation for the fields parameter in the overview section

params.name: height
params.description: (Deprecated; use height_from and height_to instead) Show images with the specified height

params.name: height_from
params.description: Show images with the specified height or larger, in pixels

params.name: height_to
params.description: Show images with the specified height or smaller, in pixels

params.name: image_type
params.description: Show images of the specified type

params.name: keyword_safe_search
params.description: Hide results with potentially unsafe keywords

params.name: language
params.description: Set query and result language (uses Accept-Language header if not set)

params.name: license
params.description: Show only images with the specified license

params.name: model
params.description: Show image results with the specified model IDs

params.name: orientation
params.description: Show image results with horizontal or vertical orientation

params.name: page
params.description: Page number

params.name: per_page
params.description: Number of results per page

params.name: people_model_released
params.description: Show images of people with a signed model release

params.name: people_age
params.description: Show images that feature people of the specified age category

params.name: people_ethnicity
params.description: Show images with people of the specified ethnicities

params.name: people_gender
params.description: Show images with people of the specified gender

params.name: people_number
params.description: Show images with the specified number of people

params.name: query
params.description: One or more search terms separated by spaces; you can use NOT to filter out images that match a term

params.name: region
params.description: Raise or lower search result rankings based on the result's relevance to a specified region; you can provide a country code or an IP address from which the API infers a country

params.name: safe

params.name: sort

params.name: spellcheck_query
params.description: Spellcheck the search query and return results on suggested spellings

params.description: Amount of detail to render in the response

params.description: (Deprecated; use width_from and width_to instead) Show images with the specified width

params.name: width_from
params.description: Show images with the specified width or larger, in pixels

params.name: width_to
params.description: Show images with the specified width or smaller, in pixels

items.docs: This endpoint searches for images. If you specify more than one search parameter, the API uses an AND condition. Array parameters can be specified multiple times; in this case, the API uses an AND or an OR condition with those values, depending on the parameter. You can also filter search terms out in the `query` parameter by prefixing the term with NOT. Free API accounts show results only from a limited library of media, not the full Shutterstock media library. Also, the number of search fields

items.docs: This endpoint lists images that have been updated in the specified time period to update content management systems (CMS) or digital asset management (DAM) systems. In most cases, use the `interval` parameter to show images that were updated recently, but you can also use the `start_date` and `end_date` parameters to specify a range of no more than three days. Do not use the `interval` parameter with either `start_date` or `end_date`.

items.docs: Deprecated; use `GET /v2/editorial/images/search` instead to search for editorial images.

Deprecated; use `GET /v2/editorial/images/categories` instead. This endpoint lists the categories that editorial images can belong to, which are separate from the categories that other types of assets can belong to.

params.name: type
params.description: Specify `addition` to return only images that were added or `edit` to return only images that were edited or deleted

params.name: date_updated_start
params.description: Show images images added, edited, or deleted after the specified date. Acceptable range is 1970-01-01T00:00:01 to 2038-01-19T00:00:00.

params.name: date_updated_end
params.description: Show images images added, edited, or deleted before the specified date. Acceptable range is 1970-01-01T00:00:01 to 2038-01-19T00:00:00.

params.name: date_taken_start
params.description: Show images that were taken on or after the specified date; use this parameter if you want recently created images from the collection instead of updated older assets

params.name: date_taken_end
params.description: Show images that were taken before the specified date

params.name: cursor
params.description: The cursor of the page with which to start fetching results; this cursor is returned from previous requests

params.description: Sort by

params.description: Show only editorial content from certain suppliers

params.name: country

params.name: per_page
params.description: Number of results per page

params.name: query
params.description: One or more search terms separated by spaces

params.name: sort

params.name: category
params.description: Show editorial video content within a certain editorial category; specify by category name

params.name: country
params.description: Show only editorial video content that is available for distribution in a certain country

params.name: supplier_code
params.description: Show only editorial video content from certain suppliers

params.name: date_start
params.description: Show only editorial video content generated on or after a specific date

params.name: date_end
params.description: Show only editorial video content generated on or before a specific date

params.name: resolution
params.description: Show only editorial video content with specific resolution

params.name: fps
params.description: Show only editorial video content generated with specific frames per second

params.name: per_page
params.description: Number of results per page

params.name: cursor
params.description: The cursor of the page with which to start fetching results; this cursor is returned from previous requests

items.docs: This endpoint searches for editorial videos. If you specify more than one search parameter, the API uses an AND condition. Array parameters can be specified multiple times; in this case, the API uses an AND or an OR condition with those values, depending on the parameter. You can also filter search terms out in the `query` parameter by prefixing the term with NOT.
This endpoint lists the categories that editorial videos can belong to, which are separate from the categories that other types of assets can belong to.

params.name: id
params.description: Editorial ID

params.name: country
params.description: Returns only if the content is available for distribution in a certain country

items.docs: This endpoint shows information about an editorial image, including a URL to a preview image and the sizes that it is available in.
This endpoint gets licenses for one or more editorial videos. You must specify the country and one or more editorial videos to license.

This endpoint uploads an image for reverse image or video search. Images must be in JPEG or PNG format. To get the search results, pass the upload ID that this endpoint returns to the GET /v2/cv/similar/images or GET /v2/cv/similar/videos endpoints. Contact us for access to this endpoint.

params.description: Language for the keywords and categories in the response

params.description: Page number

params.description: Number of results per page

params.description: Amount of detail to render in the response

params.name: client_id
params.description: Client ID (Consumer Key) of your application

params.name: realm
params.description: User type to be authorized (usually 'customer')

params.name: redirect_uri
params.description: The callback URI to send the request to after authorization; must use a host name that is registered with your application

params.name: response_type
params.description: Type of temporary authorization code that will be used to generate an access code; the only valid value is 'code'

params.name: scope
params.description: Space-separated list of scopes to be authorized

params.name: state
params.description: Unique value used by the calling app to verify the request

items.docs: This endpoint returns a redirect URI (in the 'Location' header) that the customer uses to authorize your application and, together with POST /v2/oauth/access_token, generate an access token that represents that authorization.

This endpoint returns an access token for the specified user and with the specified scopes. The token does not expire until the user changes their password. The body parameters must be encoded as form data.
